channel.title: DWS CIO View
channel.description: DWS CIO archive rebuilt from source pages with local full-text item pages.

item.title: When gas determines power
item.description: Correlations in times of energy stress

item.title: War-weary investors turn back to AI
item.description: Seven weeks of war in the Persian Gulf, traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted, and shortages of raw materials are becoming noticeable. Yet investors are betting on peace negotiations and a swift normalization.

item.title: Americas CIO View
item.description: Investing in a Fog of War and Haze of AI Transition

item.title: How oil shocks can breed demand destruction
item.description: When it comes to oil, the price spikes grab attention. Their more lasting effect may be demand that disappears - and does not fully return.

item.title: Gold in the stress test
item.description: How the precious metal behaves relative to the S&P 500 across different market phases

item.title: Energy shocks fade. Their echo does not.
item.description: When prices jump, households adjust fast. Their expectations do not.

item.title: What’s driving private-credit valuations?
item.description: Taking a closer look at what’s happening in BDCs

item.title: Oil shocks don’t bite like they used to
item.description: Decades of efficiency and electrification have defanged a once-feared macro risk — even as Middle East tensions keep markets jumpy.

item.title: Americas CIO View
item.description: Time to Turn Back?: Return to Growth or seek US equity alternatives?

item.title: U.S. and Israel strike Iran
item.description: Market impact likely hinges on the scale of oil-supply disruption
